title: The Hard Stuff! All About Bones
titleZh: 硬家伙！关于骨骼的一切

q.en: How many bones are in the adult human skeleton?
q.zh: 成年人体骨骼由多少块骨头组成？

options.en: 333 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book states the human skeleton is made up of 206 bones, from the skull at the top to the toe bones at the bottom.
explain.zh: 书中说，人体骨骼由206块骨头组成，从顶部的颅骨一直到脚趾骨。

q.en: What tissue connects muscles to bones?
q.zh: 什么组织把肌肉连接到骨头上？

explain.en: The book says many bones have muscles attached to them by tendons, while ligaments connect bones to other bones.
explain.zh: 书中说，许多骨头通过肌腱连接肌肉；而韧带是把骨头与骨头连接起来的组织。

q.en: What are sutures in the skull?

options.en: Joints between skull bones that do not move much
options.zh: 颅骨之间的接缝，几乎不活动

options.en: The soft part of the nose according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 鼻子柔软的部分（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book explains the skull bones come together at special joints called sutures, which unlike other joints don't move much.
explain.zh: 书中解释，颅骨的各块骨头在特殊的接缝处相连，这些“骨缝”不像其他关节那样能大幅活动。

q.en: What are the tiniest bones in the human body?
q.zh: 人体中最小的骨头是什么？

options.en: The bones in the toes according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 脚趾里的骨头（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The ear bones: hammer, anvil, and stirrup

explain.en: The book's Bones Bonus says the hammer, anvil, and stirrup attached to the eardrum are the tiniest bones in your body.
explain.zh: 书中“骨骼小知识”说，附着在鼓膜上的锤骨、砧骨和镫骨是人体中最小的骨头。

q.en: How many vertebrae make up the human backbone?
q.zh: 人的脊柱由多少块椎骨组成？

explain.en: The book says the backbone has thirty-three vertebrae, including seven cervical, twelve thoracic, five lumbar, five fused sacral, and the coccyx.
explain.zh: 书中说脊柱由33块椎骨组成，包括7块颈椎、12块胸椎、5块腰椎、5块融合的骶骨以及尾骨。

q.en: What is the longest, strongest, and largest bone in the human body?
q.zh: 人体中最长、最结实、最大块的骨头是哪一块？

explain.en: The book's Bones Bonus states the femur, extending from the pelvis to the knee, is the longest, strongest, and largest bone in your body.
explain.zh: 书中“骨骼小知识”指出，从骨盆延伸到膝盖的股骨是人体中最长、最结实、最大块的骨头。

q.en: Which type of joint can only bend in one direction, like a door?
q.zh: 哪一类关节只能像门一样朝一个方向弯曲？

explain.en: The book says the elbow and knee are hinge joints that can only bend in one direction, like a door opening.
explain.zh: 书中说，肘部和膝部是铰链关节，只能像门一样朝一个方向弯曲。

q.en: What does bone marrow produce inside your bones?
q.zh: 骨髓在骨头内部制造什么？

explain.en: The book says bone marrow is where the body makes blood cells, producing more than 50 billion red blood cells every day in a typical adult.
explain.zh: 书中说骨髓是身体制造血细胞的场所，普通成年人每天产生超过500亿个红细胞。

q.en: How many bones are in one human hand (wrist and hand together)?
q.zh: 一只人手（手腕加手掌）里共有多少块骨头？

options.en: 14 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book states the wrist and hand together have twenty-seven bones — more than any other part of the body (a foot has twenty-six).
explain.zh: 书中说，手腕和手加起来共有27块骨头——比身体任何部位都多（脚为26块）。

q.en: According to the book, why is it important to keep bones healthy?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，保持骨骼健康为何重要？

options.en: So they stay strong and let you move, sit, stand, and write
options.zh: 这样它们才能保持强健，让你能活动、坐立和书写

options.en: So they stop making blood cells
options.zh: 这样它们就停止制造血细胞

options.en: So they can be replaced with metal according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 这样它们才能被金属替换（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: So they grow taller than the body
options.zh: 这样它们会比身体长得更高

explain.en: The book says bones need to stay healthy to remain strong, and they give you the ability to move, sit, stand, and write — you wouldn't be you without them.
explain.zh: 书中说骨骼需要保持健康才能保持强健，并赋予你活动、坐立、书写的能力——没有骨骼就不成其为你。

prompt.en: The book explains that your bones keep growing and changing from before birth until your early twenties. Why do you think it is important for kids and teens to build strong, healthy bones, and what is one way the book suggests doing that?
prompt.zh: 书中说，人的骨骼从出生前一直到二十岁出头都在不断生长和变化。你认为儿童和青少年养成强健骨骼为何重要？书中建议的其中一个方法是什么？

en: Recall that the pelvis bones become one solid structure in the late teens or early twenties.
Think about exercise and calcium-rich foods mentioned in the book.
zh: 回想骨盆骨骼会在青少年晚期至二十岁出头融合为一块整体。
想想书中提到的运动和富含钙的食物。
